stories.headline: FERC Chair Swett: PJM may be 'too big to function,' calls July governance hearing
stories.summary: FERC Chair Laura Swett said PJM 13-state footprint covers fundamentally different regulatory structures and will convene a July technical conference on capacity, colocation, and governance reforms.

stories.headline: Constellation files 5 GW of gas, nuclear, battery into PJM interconnection queue
stories.summary: Constellation Energy submitted interconnection requests totaling roughly 5,000 MW across gas, nuclear uprate, and battery resources in PJM, citing capacity-price signals and data-center load.

stories.headline: Q1 utility earnings split: data-center bullishness collides with affordability pressure
stories.summary: Major investor-owned utilities reported mixed Q1 results as data-center load forecasts climbed while regulators and consumer advocates pushed back on customer-bill impacts.

stories.headline: NEMA projects U.S. electricity consumption up 55% by 2050
stories.summary: The National Electrical Manufacturers Association said electrification and data-center demand will drive U.S. annual electricity consumption 55% higher by 2050, well above earlier baselines.

stories.headline: Con Edison to spend $29B on NYC-area grid for electrification load
stories.summary: Con Edison announced a $29 billion transmission and distribution buildout to absorb New York-area building and transportation electrification, the latest signpost for PJM-side T&D capex pressures.

stories.headline: Mystery PAC drops $1.2M into PA-07 Democratic primary days before vote
stories.summary: A new outside group with GOP-operative ties spent more than $1.2 million in last-minute ads in the Lehigh Valley PA-07 Democratic primary, pulling national Democrats in to defend Tuesday contest.

stories.headline: Fetterman casts deciding vote against limiting Trump's Iran war
stories.summary: Sen. John Fetterman was the lone Democrat to side with Republicans on the 49-50 war-powers resolution, joined on the other side by Sens. Paul, Collins, and Murkowski.

stories.headline: PA data-center town hall blasts industry, Shapiro 'secrecy'
stories.summary: Speakers at a Wednesday-night virtual town hall described Pennsylvania data-center buildout as an onslaught enabled by Gov. Shapiro support, intensifying the political fight over siting and load-cost allocation.

stories.headline: Shapiro-AOC proxy fight roils Philadelphia PA-03 primary
stories.summary: Gov. Josh Shapiro and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have backed opposing candidates in the open Philadelphia PA-03 primary, injecting national progressive vs. governing-wing drama into the final stretch.

stories.headline: Hersheypark maintenance workers vote on strike days before May 21 opening
stories.summary: More than 200 ride mechanics, electricians, and technicians are voting on a walkout after rejecting a contract, putting Hersheypark full summer opening at risk.

stories.headline: CBC at a crossroads as GOP redistricting threatens six-plus Black-held seats
stories.summary: Following the Supreme Court Callais decision gutting Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, GOP map-redraws in South Carolina, Louisiana, Alabama, Missouri, and Tennessee could shrink the Congressional Black Caucus by six-plus members this November.

stories.headline: GOP leaders in the dark on NJ Rep. Tom Kean 70-day absence
stories.summary: Speaker Mike Johnson and his team say they have no information on the medical issue keeping NJ-07 Tom Kean off the floor since March 5; Kean staff insists he will return very soon for one of the country most competitive seats.

stories.headline: Defense spending in chaos as White House withholds Iran supplemental
stories.summary: Appropriators are stuck without details on Trump $1.5T defense request or a supplemental for the $29B Iran conflict; the Navy says it runs out of Iran-war funds in July.

stories.headline: House to vote today on Iran war-powers resolution and FY27 MilCon-VA
stories.summary: Rep. Josh Gottheimer (D-NJ) Iran war-powers resolution gets a House floor vote today alongside the FY2027 MilCon-VA bill, the chamber first FY27 appropriations measure to reach the floor.

stories.headline: Skenes carries no-hit bid into 7th; Rockies still drop Pirates 10-4
stories.summary: Paul Skenes took a no-hitter into the seventh before Colorado broke through; Mickey Moniak birthday homer, double, and ninth-inning two-run triple powered a 10-4 Rockies win, dropping the Pirates to 23-20.

stories.headline: Aaron Rodgers expected in Pittsburgh this weekend as Steelers OTAs loom May 18
stories.summary: NFL Network reports Aaron Rodgers will visit the Steelers this weekend and is likely to play in 2026, with OTAs opening Monday May 18 and the team still waiting on a formal answer.
